message:
fix(runtime): make Program::with_native_backend Unix-only (#66)

The previous code had a #[cfg(not(unix))] arm that called
TtyBackend::new(0, 0) — the headless test constructor — leaving
Windows builds with a 0×0 surface that gets clamped to 1×1 and
produces a single init-frame-then-silence pattern looking like a
hung TUI. Surfaced via cass#204 with decisive Windows forensics:
ftui emits zero log events between "launching ftui runtime" and
process exit, asciicast captures 0 bytes, and conhost prints a
single character drawn at column 1 on a 1×1 surface.

Also bit ftui-demo-showcase (#49 → PR #50). Every consumer of
ftui-runtime would rediscover this and apply the same cfg-split
at the call site, which is the wrong place for the gate.

Fix: gate Program::with_native_backend with #[cfg(unix)] and
gate AppBuilder::run_native's working impl with #[cfg(all(feature
= "native-backend", unix))]. The non-Unix run_native path now
returns io::ErrorKind::Unsupported with a message pointing
callers at AppBuilder::run() (crossterm-compat).

Closes #66

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
